name: Dragon Blood Tree
primary_sources: Socotra Archipelago World Heritage Site documentation

distinctive_features: dark red resin known as dragon's blood
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: up to 9 m (30 ft) in height and 12 m (39 ft) across the crown
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: leaves in dense rosettes at branch ends, branches divide into two sections

functional_attributes.primary_function: provides shade and reduces evaporation in arid conditions

domain_specific_functional.growth_pattern: secondary growth with scattered vascular strands

relationships.description: native to the Socotra archipelago, part of Yemen
fruits are eaten and dispersed by birds

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: national tree of Yemen, important for conservation efforts
primary_sources: Socotra Archipelago World Heritage Site documentation

contrastive_constraints: NOT a tree with continuous growth rings
NOT a tree from a non-arid environment
